steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank comes between “pages” and “a collection of Norse tales dating to 1270.” The word “from” begins the prepositional phrase “from a collection of Norse tales dating to 1270,” which modifies the noun “pages.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A preposition should not be separated from its object by punctuation. The no-punctuation version keeps “from” directly attached to its object, “a collection of Norse tales dating to 1270.” A colon, comma, or em dash after “from” would incorrectly separate the preposition from its object.

That confirms C is correct.
